creative_brief: Hero image: a real farmer's hand holding a crate of greens with a visible farm name tag. Below the fold, split-screen showing a supermarket barcode label versus a FarmThru product label with full farm provenance. Clean, warm palette. Urgency reinforced with a live waitlist counter badge above the fold.
headline: I lied about where our groceries came from
subhead: Then I found the farms willing to sell direct. The waitlist closes before launch.
hero_copy:
For years I told myself supermarket food was fine. Fresh enough. Good enough. Then I flipped over a pack of beef mince and saw no farm name. No region. Just a barcode and a use-by date.

I started asking questions. The answers were worse than I expected. Produce travelling over 2,400km before reaching the shelf. Farmers earning cents while retail prices climbed.

So we built something different. A grocery store where every product traces back to a named farm. Order online, collect from Brookvale. No middlemen inflating the price.

327 people have already joined the VIP waitlist. Spots close before we launch.

sections.heading: The problem nobody talks about at the checkout
sections.body:
Your supermarket trolley looks full. But the supply chain behind it is broken.

Produce travels up to 10x further than it needs to. Farmers sell at a loss so retailers can run specials. And you pay premium prices for food with no name, no story, no traceability.

This isn't a niche concern. It's the weekly shop for every household in Australia. The system rewards distance and anonymity. It punishes the people who actually grow your food.

sections.heading: How FarmThru fixes it in three steps
sections.body:
Step 1: We partner directly with farms across NSW. Around 15 to 20 named producers, from regenerative beef to seasonal greens.

Step 2: You order online whenever you want. No subscription. No lock-in. No membership fees. Buy in bulk and save more.

Step 3: Collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. That's it. Farm to hub to you. No unnecessary middlemen adding cost.

sections.heading: You can see every farm behind every product
sections.body:
Brookvale is our first hub: Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd. We also offer delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong.

Every product lists the farm it came from. Not a region. Not a state. The actual farm.

This is what "we'll show you where your food comes from" looks like. Not a marketing slogan. A traceable supply chain you can verify yourself.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first
sections.body:
We're opening equity crowdfunding through Birchal. This is your chance to be a partner, not just a customer.

The VIP waitlist gives you first access to invest, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A with our founding team. It costs $5, fully refundable, no commitment.

Why a waitlist? Because the first 48 hours of a crowdfunding campaign determine its momentum. We need committed people ready on day one.

The waitlist closes before launch. Once it's full, it's full. Join now or wait for whatever's left.

No commitment beyond the deposit. You decide later if you want to invest. Your $5 is refundable at any time.

sections.heading: This is bigger than one grocery run
sections.body:
Every dollar spent at FarmThru goes further for farmers. Every order proves that direct supply chains work at scale.

You're not just buying better food. You're voting for a system where values and money finally point the same direction.

Be part of it before the waitlist closes.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign will be hosted on Birchal. Investing in startups and early-stage companies carries risks, including the risk of losing your entire investment. This is not financial advice. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent financial advice if needed. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. Any investment decision should be made after reviewing the offer document available on the Birchal platform.
